mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously whenever rate changes occur.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of May 2018 indicates historically low rates
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above trend, signaling robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity. This triggers positive equity returns within 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward. The signal activates only when monthly PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k persons.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year, signaling weakening consumer demand and future earnings pressure. This negative demand signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth and inflation jointly signal supply-side constraints. When INDPRO growth slows below 1% while CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, stagflationary pressures emerge, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days as real discount rates rise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth < 1% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5%
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure regime with supply constraints
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline sharply, signaling deteriorating demand expectations and reduced future construction employment. This forward-looking demand signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market anticipates earnings headwinds in cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises sharply, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points in a 3-month window, negative equity returns follow within 20 trading days as growth expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth and retail sales growth move in tandem above trend, jointly signaling robust aggregate demand. When both GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized and RSAFS growth exceeds 3% year-over-year, positive equity returns materialize over 60 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS YoY growth > 3%
regime_hint: strong cyclical expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate rises above 2.0% while inflation remains subdued (CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2.5%), signaling monetary tightening in a low-inflation regime. This real rate increase compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as discount rates rise without offsetting earnings growth.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with contained inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 3-year window.
